figures.caption: Fig. 1. Comparison of DIW and the proposed ADIW.
TABLE I Comparison of classic Importance Weighting (IW), Dynamic IW (DIW), and the proposed Accelerated DIW (ADIW) method.
Fig. 2. Illustration of WE in ADIW. “idx” denotes the index; “Train” and “Val” denote training and validation data, respectively.
Fig. 3. Experimental results on Fashion-MNIST and CIFAR-10/100 under label noise (3 trials).
Fig. 4. Training time (minutes) under 0.4 symmetric label noise.
Fig. 5. Statistics of weight distributions on CIFAR-10 under 0.4 symmetric label noise (top: box plots; bottom: histogram plots).
TABLE VI Comparison of data transformation choices in ADIW on CIFAR-10.
TABLE VII Stage-wise breakdown of total CPU and CUDA time on CIFAR-10 (seconds; percentages in parentheses).
TABLE VIII Mean accuracy (standard deviation) in percentage on Fashion-MNIST, CIFAR-10/-100 over the last ten epochs under label noise (3 trials).
Fig. 6. Statistics of weight distributions on CIFAR-10 under 0.4 symmetric label noise (top: box plots; bottom: histogram plots).
TABLE IX End-to-end training time per epoch∗ in seconds on Fashion-MNIST (F-MNIST), CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ImageNet-1K.
